reasoning_content: We need to determine if 3937 is prime. Check divisibility by small primes. First, note 3937 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits: 3+9+3+7=22, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Ends with 7, not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*562=3934, remainder 3, so not divisible. 11: alternating sum: (3-9+3-7) = -10, not multiple of 11. 13: 13*302=3926, remainder 11, no. 17: 17*231=3927, remainder 10; 17*232=3944 too big. 19: 19*207=3933, remainder 4; 19*208=3952. 23: 23*171=3933, remainder 4; 23*172=3956. 29: 29*135=3915, remainder 22; 29*136=3944. 31: 31*127=3937? 31*127 = 31*100=3100, 31*27=837, total 3937 exactly! So 3937 = 31 * 127. Therefore, not prime. Answer: no.
